title: Further education teaching professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the academic, administrative, or auxiliary activities of kindergarten, elementary, or secondary schools. Plan, direct, or coordinate student instruction, administration, and services, as well as other research and educational activities, at postsecondary institutions, including universities, colleges, and junior and community colleges. All education administrators not listed separately. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Adapt teaching methods and instructional materials to meet students' varying needs and interests, and instruct students individually and in groups using various teaching methods, such as lectures, discussions, and demonstrations.

Prepare reports and maintain accurate student records, such as grades, attendance rolls, and training activity details, as well as prepare reports on students and activities as required by administration.
Observe and evaluate students' performance, behavior, social development, and physical health, as well as meet with other professionals to discuss individual students' needs and progress.

Plan and conduct activities for a balanced program of instruction, demonstration, and work time that provides students with opportunities to observe, question, and investigate, and prepare objectives and outlines for courses of study, following curriculum guidelines or requirements of states and schools.

Establish clear objectives for all lessons, units, and projects, and communicate those objectives to students, while ensuring the enforcement of all administration policies and rules governing students.
Prepare materials and classrooms for class activities and supervise independent or group projects, field placements, laboratory work, or other training.
Serve on faculty and school committees concerned with budgeting, curriculum revision, and course and diploma requirements, and attend staff meetings as required.
Participate in conferences, seminars, and training sessions to keep abreast of developments in the field, while integrating relevant information into training programs.

Advise students on course selection, career decisions, and other academic and vocational concerns, while guiding and counseling students with adjustments, academic problems, or special academic interests.

Collaborate with other teachers and administrators in the development, evaluation, and revision of educational programs and practices, and confer with staff to plan lessons that promote learning, following approved curricula.
Prepare, administer, and grade tests and assignments to evaluate students' progress, including the preparation and implementation of remedial programs for students requiring extra help.
Sponsor extracurricular activities, such as clubs, student organizations, and academic contests, and coordinate and direct these programs effectively.

Meet with parents and guardians to discuss their children's progress and to determine priorities for their academic and resource needs, and confer with parents or guardians to resolve behavioral and academic issues.
Keep informed about trends in education and subject matter specialties, attending professional meetings, educational conferences, and workshops to maintain and improve professional competence.

Conduct on-the-job training classes or training sessions to teach and demonstrate principles, techniques, procedures, or methods of designated subjects, while integrating vocational and academic curricula for skill development.
Evaluate curricula, teaching methods, and programs to determine their effectiveness and compliance with regulations, including establishing operational policies and procedures based on this analysis.
Recruit, hire, train, and evaluate teaching and support staff, while setting educational standards and goals to carry out effective policies and procedures.

Direct, coordinate, and evaluate the activities of personnel including support staff in educational institutions, and prepare, maintain, and oversee the preparation of reports and records related to activities and personnel.
Plan, administer, and control budgets, maintain financial records, and produce financial reports related to educational programs and institutions.
Promote partnerships with industry and secondary education institutions to enrich the educational experience and provide career opportunities for students.
Design curricular programs and instructional methods for educational, vocational, or student activity programs, ensuring they meet the needs of students and the institution.
Prepare outlines of instructional programs and training schedules, establishing course goals to ensure a comprehensive educational offering.
Use computers, audio-visual aids, and other materials to enhance educational presentations and instructional effectiveness.
Maintain accurate and complete student records as required by laws and administrative regulations, ensuring compliance and efficient management of educational data.
Review and approve new programs or recommend modifications to existing programs, preparing necessary proposals as required by educational governance.
Observe teaching methods and learning materials to evaluate curricula, and to determine areas for improvement in educational practices.

Liquid crystal display LCD video projectors
